_rationale: Corpus is now a `git_fixture` (tests/integration/cli/git/fixture) that the integration runner materializes into a fresh temp git repo (init/add/commit) before each run, instead of the `lci-go-repo` symlink to a live developer repo. `--scope wip` reads `git diff HEAD` — the live repo's tracked working tree is edited outside the test, so any uncommitted change flipped `files_changed` from 0 to N and produced flaky stable-tier mismatches (proven: dirtying one tracked file yields `summary.files_changed: stable mismatch (0 vs 1)` etc.). A freshly-committed fixture guarantees a clean tree, so the WIP diff is deterministically empty across runs and machines while still exercising the real git path. Stable tier compares the summary counts/recommendation plus the metadata refs/scope; ignore tier covers runtime-only drift: `metadata.analysis_time_ms`, `metadata.analyzed_at`, and the low-signal `summary.risk_score`.
